headline: SILVER SURGES 3% AS GOLD HOLDS NEAR $4,800: China's 17th Month of Central Bank Buying Deepens Bull Run
snippet: Spot gold held near $4,826 on April 15 while silver rallied 3.26% to $79.67, compressing the gold/silver ratio to 60.6. The People's Bank of China extended its monthly gold purchase streak to 17 consecutive months through March, with Shanghai Gold Exchange wholesale withdrawals surging 57% month-over-month to 134 tonnes.

headline: WEST RED LAKE GOLD DRILLS 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen — 904 Complex Delivers Multiple High-Grade Hits
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSX-V: WRLG) reported three high-grade intercepts in the Austin 904 Complex at Madsen Mine in Ontario's Red Lake District, headlined by 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35 metres including 388.90 g/t Au over 2.95m. The 904 Complex is a previously unmined section of the deposit; initial production is targeted for H1 2027 with the zone expected to be the primary drilling focus throughout 2026.

headline: AbraSilver EXTENDS Diablillos Gold Beyond Known Pit: 171-Metre Intercept at 0.83 g/t in Argentina
snippet: AbraSilver Resource (TSX: ABRA) drilled a 171-metre intercept grading 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag at the Oculto East target within its Diablillos project in Salta, Argentina, including a 9-metre interval at 3.46 g/t Au starting at only 79m depth. The step-out result pushes mineralization well beyond the current open-pit constrained resource envelope, adding material upside to one of Argentina's largest undeveloped silver-gold projects.

headline: G MINING SWALLOWS G2 GOLDFIELDS for C$3 Billion: Guyana Gold Hub Targets 700,000+ Oz/Year
snippet: G Mining Ventures agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal valued at approximately $2.2 billion USD (C$3B), combining the adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie deposits in Guyana into a district-scale complex targeting 700,000+ oz of gold annually at some of the lowest projected costs in the Americas. The 72% premium to G2's 30-day VWAP and over $1 billion in projected synergies illustrates how elevated gold prices are accelerating consolidation among mid-tier producers.

headline: COPPER HOLDS NEAR $6/LB AT 6-WEEK HIGHS: Iran Ceasefire Extension + Chile Supply Disruptions Keep Metal Bid
snippet: Copper futures traded near $6.07 per pound on April 15, holding six-week highs as U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension talks reduced risk-off sentiment while simultaneous supply disruptions in Chile, Indonesia, and Peru kept physical market tightness intact. Goldman Sachs trimmed its 2026 copper forecast to $12,650/t average from $12,850, but copper is still up nearly 30% year-over-year.

headline: DOLLAR WEAKNESS AND IRAN TALKS Lift Metals Across the Board — Asian Markets Lead Gold and Silver Higher
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian market wrap identifies a weakening U.S. dollar and expanding U.S.-Iran nuclear diplomacy as the twin engines driving gold and silver higher during Asian trading hours. The gold/silver ratio is contracting sharply from its April peak above 105 — a historically bullish signal that has preceded significant silver outperformance in prior precious metals cycles.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790 TONNES of Silver in Early 2026 — Solar Boom Driving Unprecedented Industrial Demand
snippet: China's silver imports surged past 790 tonnes in the first months of 2026, driven primarily by domestic solar panel manufacturing which consumes silver in photovoltaic cells at a rate outpacing mine supply growth. The Silver Institute projects a sixth consecutive structural supply deficit of approximately 67 million ounces in 2026, putting persistent upward pressure on a market where industrial demand has become the dominant price driver.

headline: THE ECONOMISTS KEEP LOSING: Gold at $4,800 Has Made Fools of Every Mainstream Price Prediction Since 2020
snippet: Money Metals' April 14 commentary traces the unbroken streak of mainstream economists under-calling gold — from dismissals at $1,800 through confident ceiling calls at $2,500, $3,000, and $4,000, each of which was breached with ease. The piece argues that the structural case (fiscal expansion, de-dollarization, central bank accumulation) remains fully intact and that each analyst capitulation has historically marked an accumulation opportunity, not a top.

headline: SERABI GOLD Q1 2026: 12,042 Oz (+20% YoY), Now DEBT-FREE With $64M Cash as Small Producers Catch the Bull
snippet: Serabi Gold (AIM: SRB, TSX: SBI) produced 12,042 ounces of gold in Q1 2026, a 20% increase year-over-year, while paying off all remaining bank debt and ending the quarter with $64.4 million in cash versus $49.2 million at year-end. The Brazil-focused junior maintained full-year guidance of 53,000–57,000 oz and highlighted ongoing Palito mill expansion work expected to push production toward the upper end of that range.

headline: CAMECO UP 27% YTD: Energy Supercycle Analysts Say Uranium Miner Has Years of Structural Upside Ahead
snippet: Motley Fool's April 13 analysis argues Cameco is among the best-positioned uranium producers for a multi-year energy supercycle, with U3O8 spot at $85.45/lb and the company's 49% Westinghouse stake adding high-margin nuclear services revenue on top of its core mining cash flows. Cameco management has flagged long-term contract price ceilings of $140–$150/lb, implying significant upside from current spot levels as AI data center buildout accelerates reactor demand.

headline: GOLD'S HISTORIC FLIGHT: April Surge Signals Deepening Investor Anxiety Over Fiscal Deficits and Monetary Policy
snippet: With gold near $4,800, MarketMinute's April 13 analysis frames the move as a systemic confidence crisis rather than a speculative bubble — driven by expanding government deficits, declining dollar reserve share, and institutional reallocation away from sovereign bonds. JP Morgan and Goldman Sachs now see gold trading in the $4,000–$6,300 range over the medium term, a forecast range that would have been dismissed as fringe commentary just two years ago.
